summary: roles.kernel enforces explicit raw/sanitized/native assistant-turn stages and now consumes a KernelOne session continuity engine; CLI, runtime helper, TUI, and legacy streaming hosts all converge on structured continuity packs instead of replaying stale raw history.

tests.purpose: Protect KernelOne continuity-pack generation, reserved-key filtering, and continuity projection semantics.
Protect runtime helper hosts so local history is projected through the canonical continuity engine.
Protect legacy streaming dialogue so missing session_id creates a fresh ad-hoc session instead of reusing stale sessions.
Protect explicit fresh ad-hoc session creation semantics in roles.session.
Protect raw_content vs clean_content vs native_tool_calls stage boundaries.
Protect turn-engine policy convergence and repeated tool-cycle stop conditions.
Protect stream fallback parsing, tool-loop execution, and transcript behavior.
Protect summarize/history compaction semantics and continuity-summary notifications.
Protect fresh-session defaults, session continuity memory, and prompt-context filtering in role CLI hosts.
Protect linkage between verification cards, ADRs, debt register, and verify pack.
Protect StreamEventType parity across shared contracts, engine contracts, and executor imports.

contracts: AssistantTurnArtifacts.raw_content (str) is the only textual input allowed to reach tool-call parsing (Chief Architect Ruling #2).
AssistantTurnArtifacts.clean_content (str) is the only assistant text allowed to reach transcript persistence.
SanitizedOutput = NewType('SanitizedOutput', str) is the post-sanitization type used for user-visible output (public/transcript_ir.py, per Chief Architect Ruling #2).
ParsedToolPlan and TranscriptAppendRequest are explicit dataclasses in public/transcript_ir.py (Chief Architect Ruling #2).
transcript_ir.py is public (roles.kernel.public.transcript_ir), not internal; external consumers must import from the public path (Chief Architect Ruling #3).
native_tool_calls remain the primary structured stream source; raw_content is the textual fallback.
Older resumed dialogue must be compacted into continuity summaries; raw history cannot be injected twice through both history and context_override.

residual_risks: raw_content is typed as str (not a dedicated wrapper), so downstream consumers can still pass unsanitized text if they bypass AssistantTurnArtifacts construction.
internal/transcript_ir.py is now a compatibility re-export shim; new code must import from public/transcript_ir.py to be compliant with Chief Architect Ruling #3.
KernelOne llm contract re-exports are still manual, so future shared contract additions must extend the parity gate.
Session continuity packs still use deterministic heuristics today; future tuning must preserve the single continuity-memory data model instead of adding a second memory path.
